title: 1.1: Identifies the routing cache structure with its per-organization design (e.g., CacheSegmentedTree with a map of org IDs to routing trees)
1.2: Explains the synchronization mechanism that maintains coherence during concurrent access (e.g., sync.RWMutex protecting the routing map)
1.4: Explains what determines which routing view is authoritative at any instant (e.g., the view in the map when a read lock is acquired)
1.5: States whether incomplete or stale routes can be exposed during overlapping operations (e.g., no partial routes exposed due to atomic replacement)
1.7: Describes the background refresh mechanism with its timing (e.g., updatePeriodically runs periodically to refresh routing trees)
